rulings.Case Title: MARGARET SUE KAUFMAN VS. ROBIN GOLDMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 5, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT ROBIN GOLDMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET SUE KAUFMAN VS. ROBIN GOLDMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 5, 2010, line 8, PLAINTIFF MARGARET KAUFMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion granted, plaintiff shifts the burden and defendant fails to raise a triable issue of material fact. Defendant signed the settlement agreement and agreed to move out. (Plaintiff's exhibit 2, paragraph 3.1.) Defendant did not move out as agreed (UMF6). No authority that this breach of contract action is subject to the SFRO. No evidence presented that waiver, estoppel, or any other affirmative defense raised in plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication has merit or would provide a defense to the issue of possession. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DOREEN FINGER VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 5, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION, DAVID GOSSAGE, G. HARRINGTON, DAVID SANDLER, PATRICK PETERSON, GERALD KELLY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 8th (assault), 9th (battery), 11th (negligent infliction of emotional distress), and 12th (Defamation) causes of action. 8th, 9th, & 12th time-barred. 11th cause of action barred by worker's compensation exclusivity provision. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 10th cause of action to plead compliance with Tort Claims Act and to allege conduct within the statute of limitations. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBHORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. PATRICK AMANZE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Deft'S Petition For Relief From Forfeiture, Ccp 1179; Memorandum Of P/A; Declarations And Exhibits A-F In Support Thereof; Deft'S Verification; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 5, 2010, line 20, DEFENDANT PATRICK AMANZE Notice Of Defendant's Petition For Relief From Forfeiture, CCP 1179. Motion granted. Defendant must immediately pay all rents and damages due on the premises through January 31, 2010. Additionally, defendant must pay plaintiffs' attorney fees in the amount of $1,725.65 and costs of $547.50 by January 31, 2011. =(302/CWW)
